data.title: Ethereum Developer Tim Beiko Shifts Focus to Exploring Frontier Applications
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Ethereum core developer Tim Beiko announced a shift in his focus from primary development to exploring frontier applications of Ethereum in the coming months. His immediate priority is ensuring a smooth transition for AllCoreDevs and fulfilling his responsibilities at the Ethereum Foundation. Beiko will remain at the foundation as a protocol advisor, collaborating with the foundation and cluster leadership to establish a supportive organizational structure for ecosystem participation.

Beiko emphasized that the Ethereum protocol is nearing its final stages, yet the potential of a permissionless, scalable, economically secure, and low-cost verifiable world computer is only beginning to be realized. He aims to explore what can uniquely exist on Ethereum, how to leverage its distinctive features, and how to make Ethereum not only sufficient but essential to the world.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $93.8151 million yesterday, marking the third consecutive day of net outflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $93.8151 million yesterday (January 9th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was BlackRock ETF ETHA, with a net outflow of $83.7783 million. ETHA's historical total net inflow has reached $12.718 billion. This was followed by Grayscale Ethereum Trust ETF ETHE, with a single-day net outflow of $10.0368 million. ETHE's historical total net outflow has reached $5.141 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs is $18.699 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of total Ethereum market capitalization) of 5.04%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $12.434 billion.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $250 million yesterday, marking the fourth consecutive day of net outflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $250 million yesterday (January 9th, Eastern Time). The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the Fidelity ETF (FBTC), with a net inflow of $7.8655 million. FBTC's historical total net inflow has reached $11.722 billion. The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was the BlackRock ETF (IBIT), with a net outflow of $252 million. IBIT's historical total net inflow has reached $62.406 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $116.857 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) of 6.48%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $56.403 billion.

data.title: U.S. Bitcoin Spot ETF Experiences Significant Outflows
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on January 10, monitoring by Farside Investors revealed a net outflow of $250 million from U.S. Bitcoin spot ETFs the previous day. The breakdown of the outflows includes BlackRock's IBIT, which saw a decrease of $252 million, while Fidelity's FBTC experienced an inflow of $7.9 million. Bitwise's BITB recorded an outflow of $5.9 million.

data.title: A whale deposited $12.06 million USDC into Hyperliquid and purchased 480,000 HYPE tokens.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale (0x23Af...E2C0) deposited $1 million USDC into Hyperliquid to increase its HYPE holdings. Since December 17, 2025, this whale has cumulatively deposited $12.06 million USDC into Hyperliquid and purchased 480,997 HYPE tokens at a price of $25.07.

data.title: Binance Alpha will list CharacterX (CAI) on January 12th.
data.description: Binance Alpha will list CharacterX (CAI) on January 12th. Eligible users can claim a CAI airdrop using their Binance Alpha Points on the Alpha event page after trading begins. Specific rules and further details will be announced separately. Users can follow Binance's official channels for updates.

data.title: Senators push for X and Grok app removal from Apple and Google app stores
data.description:
Three Democratic senators sent a letter Friday asking Apple and Google to pull the X and Grok applications from their app stores until Elon Musk stops users from making and sharing explicit images without consent and material showing child sexual abuse. Senators Ron Wyden from Oregon, Ed Markey from Massachusetts, and Ben Ray Luján from […]

data.title: High-Leverage Crypto Positions Face Significant Losses
data.description:
According to Odaily, Hyperbot data reveals that a 'Strategy Counterparty' initiated a 20x leveraged long position on XRP this morning, resulting in an overall unrealized loss of approximately $2.25 million.

The breakdown of the positions includes a 15x leveraged long position on Bitcoin with 1,699 BTC at an entry price of $90,801, currently facing an unrealized loss of $480,655. Additionally, a 15x leveraged long position on Ethereum involves 33,063 ETH at an entry price of $3,099, with an unrealized loss of $597,245.

Furthermore, a 20x leveraged long position on Solana comprises 336,109 SOL at an entry price of $138, resulting in an unrealized loss of $974,221. Lastly, the 20x leveraged long position on XRP includes 3,777,318 XRP at an entry price of $2.13, with an unrealized loss of $196,956.

data.title: The "Strategy counterparty" opened a new long position in XRP with 20x leverage, and the overall position is currently showing a floating loss of approximately $2.25 million.
data.description: Hyperbot data shows that the "Strategy counterparty" opened a new 20x leveraged XRP long position this morning, with the overall position currently showing a floating loss of approximately $2.25 million. Specifically: 1. A 15x leveraged Bitcoin long position of 1,699 BTC, with an average entry price of $90,801, currently showing a floating loss of $480,655; 2. A 15x leveraged Ethereum long position of 33,063 ETH, with an average entry price of $3,099, currently showing a floating loss of $597,245; 3. A 20x leveraged SOL long position of 336,109 SOL, with an average entry price of $138, currently showing a floating loss of $974,221; 4. A 20x leveraged XRP long position of 3,777,318 XRP, with an average entry price of $2.13, currently showing a floating loss of $196,956.

data.title: A member of the WLFI Wallet team claims that USD1 will become one of the top three stablecoins within two months.
data.description: A user named Dylan_0x (@0xDylan_), suspected to be a member of the WLFI Wallet team, posted on the X platform, stating, "Within two months, USD1 will be among the top three stablecoins; this is not speculation." It is understood that WLFI announced yesterday that it will soon release a series of announcements regarding its ecosystem and the continued growth of USD1. CoinGecko data shows that USDT and USDC currently rank second and third in stablecoin market capitalization with $186.78 billion and $74.77 billion respectively, USDS ranks third with a market capitalization of approximately $9.78 billion, while USD1 currently has a market capitalization of approximately $3.43 billion, ranking seventh among stablecoins.

data.title: Regulatory Improvements Key to Institutional Adoption of Crypto Assets
data.description:
According to Odaily, Goldman Sachs analysts, including James Yaro, have highlighted the importance of regulatory improvements in driving institutional adoption of crypto assets. This development is particularly beneficial for both buy-side and sell-side financial institutions and is expected to foster new applications for crypto assets beyond trading. The analysts emphasized that the U.S. Clarity Act, currently progressing through Congress, serves as a crucial catalyst.

The report suggests that the Clarity Act will establish a clear regulatory framework for tokenized assets and decentralized finance (DeFi), delineating the responsibilities of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC). This clarity is essential for unlocking institutional capital and encouraging compliant participation. Goldman Sachs cautions that the act needs to be passed by the first half of 2026 to avoid potential delays due to the U.S. midterm elections in November.

Previously, Tim Scott, the Republican chairman of the Senate Banking Committee, indicated that the committee would soon revise the Clarity Act and move it to the voting stage. Industry experts also noted that while market adjustments towards the end of 2025 might slow short-term adoption, successful implementation of the act could significantly accelerate genuine institutional entry.
